name: SIRT2 and NMN increased BubR1 biology and extended BubR1 hypomorphic mouse lifespan
summary: SIRT2 and NAD biology provide a more druggable upstream route into BubR1 abundance, but the lifespan evidence remains in BubR1-deficient mice.
statement: SIRT2 maintained BubR1 abundance through lysine-668 deacetylation; SIRT2 overexpression or NMN treatment increased BubR1 abundance, and SIRT2 overexpression increased median lifespan in BubR1 hypomorphic mice.
population_or_model: BubR1 hypomorphic mice, SIRT2 transgenic mice, and mouse embryonic fibroblasts
time_horizon: Mouse lifespan and mechanistic cell/tissue assays
quantitative_note: The source reports increased median lifespan in BubR1 hypomorphic animals, with a greater effect in male mice.
caveats: The lifespan result is in a BubR1 hypomorphic disease model rather than naturally aged wild-type humans.
NMN is upstream and pleiotropic; the record should not be read as direct evidence that NAD precursors improve human chromosome surveillance.
SIRT2 modulation may have context-dependent cancer and cell-cycle effects.
